external_auth_security_posture_desc: Identities are matched by provider issuer and subject; email auto-linking must be explicitly enabled.
external_auth_providers_empty_desc: Create an external auth provider before the sign-in page can show an external sign-in entry.
external_auth_provider_delete_desc: This removes the provider configuration and its external identity links. Local users are not deleted.
external_auth_provider_created_callback_: {{name}} has been created. Register this redirect URI in the identity provider, then return here to test sign-in.
external_auth_provider_test_scope_hint: OIDC, Google, and Microsoft test discovery metadata and JWKS reachability. Generic OAuth2 and GitHub test endpoint configuration only; client credentials require a real sign-in.
external_auth_provider_dialog_desc: Configure identity-provider connection details, client credentials, and account linking policy. AsterDrive generates the callback URL; register it with the identity provider.
external_auth_provider_kind_generic_oaut: Authorization-code OAuth2 sign-in through manually configured authorization, token, and userinfo endpoints.
external_auth_provider_kind_github_descr: GitHub OAuth App sign-in with verified primary email fetched from the GitHub emails API.
external_auth_provider_kind_google_descr: Google OpenID Connect sign-in with fixed Google Accounts issuer, discovery, and standard email_verified semantics.
external_auth_provider_kind_microsoft_de: Microsoft Entra ID / Microsoft Account OpenID Connect sign-in with tenant-derived issuer and standard ID token identity semantics.
external_auth_provider_kind_oidc_descrip: Standards-based sign-in through OIDC discovery, authorization code flow, PKCE, nonce validation, and ID token verification.
external_auth_provider_wizard_choose_typ: Select the protocol family first. Prefer OIDC when the provider supports it; use dedicated GitHub, Google, and Microsoft types for those platforms; Generic OAuth2 covers providers that expose userinfo without ID tokens.
external_auth_provider_wizard_step_type_: Pick the single sign-on backend that this provider uses.
external_auth_provider_wizard_step_conne: Name the provider and register the generated redirect URI with the identity provider.
external_auth_provider_wizard_step_rules: Review standard claim defaults, email-domain restrictions, and account creation/linking behavior.
external_auth_provider_wizard_required: Fill in the required provider identity and connection fields before continuing.
external_auth_provider_identity_desc: The redirect URI is generated after saving. OIDC uses issuer discovery; Google uses a fixed OIDC issuer; Microsoft derives the issuer from tenant; GitHub uses fixed OAuth endpoints; Generic OAuth2 uses manual endpoint URLs.
external_auth_provider_github_fixed_desc: The GitHub provider automatically uses GitHub OAuth App authorization, token, user, and user-emails endpoints. Enter the Client ID / Secret here, then register the redirect URI in GitHub.
external_auth_provider_github_email_desc: GitHub /user can omit private email addresses. AsterDrive also reads /user/emails and only accepts an email with primary=true and verified=true.
external_auth_provider_google_fixed_desc: The Google provider uses Google Accounts discovery and issuer. Enter the Client ID / Secret here, then register the redirect URI in the Google OAuth client.
external_auth_provider_google_email_desc: Google OIDC exposes email verification through the ID token email_verified claim. Account links always use the stable sub claim, not email.
external_auth_provider_microsoft_fixed_d: The Microsoft provider derives the v2.0 issuer from tenant and uses discovery for authorization, token, and JWKS endpoints. Enter tenant, Client ID / Secret, then register the redirect URI in the app registration.
external_auth_provider_microsoft_tenant_: Match the Microsoft app registration Supported account types. Use consumers for personal accounts, organizations for work or school accounts, and common when both are allowed.

Enter a concrete tenant ID or a full issuer URL, for example https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/v2.0. Microsoft Graph permissions are later OAuth authorization and should not be added to login scopes.
external_auth_provider_microsoft_email_d: Microsoft email claims are not treated like GitHub verified primary emails. Missing or unconfirmed email follows the existing email-verification / account-binding flow.
external_auth_provider_rules_desc: Blank fields use provider defaults. GitHub, Google, and Microsoft use fixed field semantics. For non-standard userinfo responses, use claim names, dotted paths, or JSON pointers, and optionally restrict which email domains can sign in.
external_auth_provider_key_hint: Generated on save and used in sign-in and redirect URI paths. It cannot be edited manually.
external_auth_provider_icon_url_hint: Optional. Use an absolute HTTPS URL or a root-relative path. The sign-in page falls back to the SSO type icon when this is blank or the image fails to load.
external_auth_provider_secret_keep_place: Leave blank to keep the existing secret
external_auth_provider_secret_keep_hint: Keep the redacted value to preserve the existing secret. Clear it and save to remove the secret; enter a new value to replace it.
external_auth_provider_secret_hint: Optional. Public clients can leave it blank; confidential clients should use the provider-issued secret.
external_auth_provider_callback_url_hint: Register this URL in the identity provider's redirect URI / callback URL allowlist. If multiple public site origins are configured, use the origin that external users actually visit.
external_auth_provider_scopes_hint: Leave blank to use this provider type's default scopes. Google / Microsoft default to openid profile email; GitHub defaults to read:user user:email; OIDC keeps openid automatically; Generic OAuth2 defaults to openid email profile.
external_auth_provider_allowed_domains_h: Separate domains with commas or new lines. Enter example.com to allow only that email domain; leave blank for no restriction.
external_auth_provider_github_claims_des: The GitHub provider reads id, login, and name from /user. It does not trust /user.email; it filters /user/emails for the verified primary address.
external_auth_provider_google_claims_des: The Google provider uses sub as the external identity key and reads name, email, email_verified, and picture from the ID token. Email can change and is not used as the identity key.
external_auth_provider_microsoft_claims_: The Microsoft provider uses sub as the external identity key and reads name and email from the ID token. Email is not treated as a verified primary email by default.
external_auth_provider_enabled_desc: When disabled, it is hidden from the sign-in page and existing callbacks are rejected.
external_auth_provider_require_email_ver: When enabled, external identities without a verified email from the identity provider cannot sign in through this provider.
external_auth_provider_auto_link_desc: Only links when the provider returns email_verified=true and a unique local email matches.
external_auth_provider_auto_provision_de: Unlinked identities can create standard users. Disable this to require an existing link or email auto-link.
